stem: An investor deposits $10,000 into an account that earns 8% annual interest compounded once per year. After 2 years, the investor withdraws all the money. How much does the investor withdraw?

distractorRationale.A: Student used simple interest instead of compound: $10,000 × 0.08 × 2 = $1,600, total = $11,600.
distractorRationale.C: Student used 10% per year instead of 8%: $10,000 × 1.10 × 1.10 = $12,100, but more likely doubled the rate: $10,000 × 1.20 = $12,000.
distractorRationale.D: Student computed only one year of interest: $10,000 × 1.08 = $10,800.
